summary: Fast, high‑energy show with standout performances of Jack Straw, Friend of the Devil and Let It Grow; crisp mix highlights a very defined Phil bass line.
review: Both reviewers rave about the blistering pace and intensity of this late‑summer ’83 concert, singling out Jack Straw, Friend of the Devil, Let It Grow, Samson & Terrapin Station, The Other One and Sweet Baby Blue as high points.  The recording is praised for a very good mix with Phil’s bass especially clear, suggesting a clean source, though the exact source type isn’t specified.

ai_rating.rationale: Two detailed, highly positive reviews highlight an excellent performance and a very good mix; limited review count keeps confidence low.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Mix described as very good with Phil’s bass defined; no complaints about noise or drop‑outs.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Show runs at breakneck speed, delivering high‑energy first set and a concise but intense second set; the fast pacing is noted as almost too quick at points but keeps excitement high.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for being front‑and‑center alongside lyricist Robert Hunter in the second review.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is very defined and sits clearly in the mix, highlighted by reviewers.
